agents.name: Chat
agents.description: General conversation with document retrieval (RAG). Fast, personality-first chat that can read your files and answer questions about them — all locally.
agents.author: AMD
agents.readme:
# Chat Agent

The Chat agent is GAIA's general-purpose conversational agent. It combines fast, personality-first chat with on-device document retrieval (RAG), so you can drop in a PDF and ask questions about it without anything leaving your machine.

## What it does

- Natural conversation backed by a local LLM
- Document Q&A over PDFs and text files via RAG
- Shell and file tools for light local automation
- Multiple prompt profiles (chat, doc, file, data)

## Usage

```bash
gaia chat
```

Or launch the desktop Agent UI:

```bash
gaia chat --ui
```

Everything runs locally on AMD Ryzen AI hardware — no API keys, no cloud.

agents.name: Code
agents.description: Code generation with orchestration, validators, and file I/O. Plans a task, writes files, and validates the result against your project.
agents.author: AMD
agents.readme:
# Code Agent

The Code agent generates production-ready code with a plan → write → validate loop. It orchestrates file I/O and shell tools, then checks its own output against your project conventions.

## Usage

```bash
gaia-code
```

## Features

- Multi-step orchestration with validators
- Semantic code search (FAISS) over your repo
- Reads, writes, and edits files in place
- Runs entirely on local hardware

agents.name: Document Q&A
agents.description: Standalone document question-answering with RAG. Index PDFs and ask grounded questions with citations.
agents.author: AMD
agents.readme:
# Document Q&A Agent

A focused agent for asking grounded questions across a set of documents. Indexes PDFs locally and answers with retrieval-augmented generation.

## Features

- Local PDF indexing and chunking
- Grounded answers with source citations
- No data leaves your machine

agents.name: Analyst
agents.description: Structured data analysis over CSV/Excel using scratchpad SQL tables. Ask questions in plain English; get tables and charts back.
agents.author: AMD
agents.readme:
# Analyst Agent

The Analyst agent turns plain-English questions into SQL over your spreadsheets. It loads CSV/Excel into scratchpad tables and runs queries on the fly.

## Usage

```bash
gaia analyze
```

agents.name: Browser
agents.description: Web research agent — search the web, fetch pages, and download content. Summarizes what it finds locally.
agents.author: AMD
agents.readme:
# Browser Agent

Web research without leaving your terminal. The Browser agent searches the web, fetches pages, and downloads content, then summarizes findings with a local LLM.

## Usage

```bash
gaia browse
```

agents.name: File I/O
agents.description: Read, write, and edit files on disk through a conversational interface. The building block for local file automation.
agents.author: AMD
agents.readme:
# File I/O Agent

A minimal agent that reads, writes, and edits files through natural language. Useful on its own and as a building block for larger workflows.

agents.name: Email Triage
agents.description: Triage and summarize Gmail inboxes with local inference. Requires the Google connector.
agents.author: AMD
agents.readme:
# Email Triage Agent

Keeps your inbox under control with local inference. Connects to Gmail via the Google connector and summarizes, categorizes, and prioritizes mail.

## Usage

```bash
gaia email
```

Requires the Google connector — set it up with `gaia connectors`.

agents.name: Summarizer
agents.description: Summarize documents and long text into concise briefs. The simplest agent — a great starting point.
agents.author: AMD
agents.readme:
# Summarizer Agent

Turns long documents and text into concise summaries. Lightweight and fast — a good first agent to try.

## Usage

```bash
gaia summarize
```

agents.name: Jira
agents.description: Manage Jira issues from natural language — generate JQL, create and update issues, plan sprints.
agents.author: AMD
agents.readme:
# Jira Agent

Drive Jira from plain English. Generates JQL, creates and updates issues, and helps with sprint planning through the Atlassian integration.

## Usage

```bash
gaia jira
```

agents.name: Blender
agents.description: Automate Blender 3D scenes with Python scripting via the Blender MCP server. Procedural modeling from natural language.
agents.author: AMD
agents.readme:
# Blender Agent

Automates 3D scene creation in Blender through its MCP server. Describe a scene and the agent scripts it procedurally.

## Usage

```bash
gaia blender
```

agents.name: Docker
agents.description: Manage containers and images conversationally — build, run, inspect, and clean up Docker resources.
agents.author: AMD
agents.readme:
# Docker Agent

Manages Docker containers and images through natural language. Build, run, inspect, and prune resources without memorizing flags.

## Usage

```bash
gaia docker
```

agents.name: Stable Diffusion
agents.description: Generate images locally with Stable Diffusion XL Turbo on the AMD iGPU. Prompt to PNG in seconds, fully offline.
agents.author: AMD
agents.readme:
# Stable Diffusion Agent

Generates images locally with SDXL Turbo on the AMD iGPU. No cloud, no API keys — prompt to PNG in seconds.

## Usage

```bash
gaia sd
```

agents.name: Medical Intake
agents.description: Process medical intake forms with a vision LLM. Extracts structured data from scanned documents — HIPAA-friendly, fully local.
agents.author: AMD
agents.readme:
# Medical Intake Agent

Extracts structured data from scanned medical forms using a local vision LLM. Because everything runs on-device, sensitive records never leave the machine.

## Usage

```bash
gaia-emr
```

agents.name: Builder
agents.description: Scaffold new GAIA agents from templates. Generates the package structure, manifest, and boilerplate so you can start building.
agents.author: AMD
agents.readme:
# Builder Agent

Scaffolds new GAIA agents from templates — package layout, `gaia-agent.yaml` manifest, and boilerplate included. The fastest way to start a new agent.

agents.name: Routing
agents.description: Intelligently routes a request to the best-fit agent. The dispatcher behind multi-agent setups.
agents.author: AMD
agents.readme:
# Routing Agent

Picks the right agent for a given request and dispatches to it. The coordination layer for multi-agent workflows.

agents.name: Telegram Bridge
agents.description: Community-built messaging adapter that bridges a Telegram bot to your local agents.
agents.author: community
agents.readme:
# Telegram Bridge (community)

Bridges a Telegram bot to your local GAIA agents. Built and maintained by the community.

> **Community tier:** this agent is publisher-signed but not reviewed by AMD. Install with the usual caution for third-party software.

agents.name: Native Summarizer (C++)
agents.description: Experimental C++ summarization agent that runs as a native subprocess for minimal overhead.
agents.author: community
agents.readme:
# Native Summarizer (C++, experimental)

A native C++ summarization agent launched as a subprocess over JSON-RPC. Demonstrates the native agent path with minimal runtime overhead.

> **Experimental tier:** opt-in only. Native agents run outside the Python sandbox — review the source before installing.

agents.name: Code (legacy)
agents.description: Deprecated single-shot code generator. Use the Code agent instead.
agents.author: AMD
agents.deprecation_message: Superseded by the Code agent, which adds orchestration and validators. Install `code` instead.
agents.readme:
# Code (legacy)

> **Deprecated.** This agent is superseded by the [Code](/hub/code) agent. It remains listed for existing installs but receives no further updates.
